comment_text:
Been  Cowboys fan since 1971, I was 12 years old. Roger Staubach, Bob Lilly, Drew Pearson. Always went to the playoffs. New era with the JJs! But their new summer camp was 10 mins from my house! I took my kids and others in the neighborhood! It was fun! 
I was intrigued with the Netflix series. Some stuff I didn’t want to know, but I really blame Jerry Jones for the division with Jimmy Johnson. He had no business coming on the field during games. He had no businesses at camp. But Jimmy, I don’t see how he could leave that team. I always said I could have coached that team to the Super Bowl but that’s not a dig on Jimmy. He built the perfect team in every way. It was Jimmy’s team that won in 1995. I’ll say one thing. We haven’t been back to the Super Bowl since. Says a lot! 💙🌟💙

comment_text:
It was ok, if you'd watch the America's team vids of all their SB season and yearbook films of the same years you've seen 75% of this one already. This one explores things like Barry Switzer more as a person and how much the players did like him when you thought they didn't. The Irvin trial, which had totally forgotten about, they tried to act like it was the 2nd coming of the OJ tral and it wasn't, and it did paint kind of picture that I felt back then too that Jimmy was also to blame for his departure. It did showcase the lack of accountability the players had for their own actions, and how their families let stuff fly. It does make you respect Jones business sense that lead to the NFL being what it is today, but he's still way too obsessed with who should take credit for the Herchel Walker trade. Let it go Jerry, half your fanbase has no clue about the Herchel Walker trade.

Was it trying to be the last dance? Maybe, but it wasn't. The difference was there wasn't really any documentaries like the last dance talking about those Bulls teams, and we had had several already lesser ones about these cowboys.

video_title: America's Team: The Gambler and His Cowboys | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: Well, I think the Cowboys team won back in the '90s because they had the best players, period. Jimmy Johnson was OK, but not an excellent coach at all - he was just the father figure those guys needed. The team had amazing chemistry; Unfortunately, as it happens all the time, there was an expiration date, and all those drugs and sex scandals, I mean... hello... kind of sped up the process. There won´t be another dynasty in the NFL because now technology plays a big role in finding trends and suggesting playbooks - the human factor is just one thing that is used with technology. The NFL teams kind of all play the same game, just like soccer with the globalizarion and countries having local teams with almost no locals, only foreigners. In the NFL case, they start the recruiting process in high school and might just work that poor kid too much that when he gets to the NFL, he´s already burned out. As to coaches, they are just for show -- players - good players - and their chemistry on a team is what makes the team a winner.

comment_text: Jones' battle is with God (Man's ego v our Creator) and the 90's team and every team after that has fallen short of their goal each year. When will he give God the credit for the outcomes in his life? His need for recognition, praise, and credit of the teams' successes does not belong to him and his comment of 500 coaches could achieve what  Jimmy was accomplishing with the team in the 90s was a lie, but we haven't heard Jones retract that statement yet. Now it has become more like a curse to the team because the man will STILL not say out loud that he was wrong.  He has only had 8 coaches since he made that statement, so we only have 492 coaches to go.... I wish the man would wake up and realize that he is not a god.  Being a fan of the team since the 70's, I stopped watching them  in 2016-2017 (after Prescots fist year in the league) because I realized Jones stilled believed he (and his money) could make & sustain  a Championship team. At this point I am tired of watching the man repeat the same mistakes year after year. How long will it take him to surrender to God? He can't have an American team without our Creator.
